reasoning: The historical data clearly shows the Federal Reserve is actively running off its MBS holdings (Quantitative Tightening), leading to a persistent downward trend in the WSHOMCB series. Furthermore, the weekly pattern indicates that the value typically stays strictly flat between the last Wednesday of one month and the first Wednesday of the next month. In all recent months (e.g., Feb 25 to Mar 4, Jan 28 to Feb 4, Dec 31 to Jan 7), the MBS holdings experienced exactly zero change. Occasional minor increases due to settlement timings have occurred roughly twice in the last 30 weeks (around 6% of the time) but never during the first week transition. Given the strong structural downward pressure and the consistent zero-change pattern for this specific week of the month, the probability of a strict positive increase occurring on April 1, 2026 (the actual observation falling on the April 5th resolution date compared to March 29th) is very low.
